page.title: Vascular endothelial growth factor
page.text:
Vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) is a [[signal protein]] produced by cells that stimulates the formation of blood vessels. It plays a role in both physiological and pathological angiogenesis.
VEGF is a key driver of tumor angiogenesis, and drugs that block VEGF signaling, such as bevacizumab, are used to treat certain cancers.
== History == The VEGF protein was first purified in 1989.
[[Category:Cancer research]] [[Category:Angiogenesis]] [[Category:Proteins]]

page.title: The Selfish Gene
page.text:
The Selfish Gene is a 1976 book by ethologist Richard Dawkins that promotes the gene-centered view of evolution.
[[Category:Books by Richard Dawkins]] [[Category:Popular science books]] [[Category:Cancer research]]

page.title: Apoptosis
page.text:
Apoptosis is a form of [[programmed cell death]] that occurs in multicellular organisms. Biochemical events lead to characteristic cell changes and death.
In cancer, evasion of apoptosis is a hallmark that allows malignant cells to survive despite DNA damage.
[[File:Apoptosis diagram.png|thumb|Apoptosis stages]]
[[Category:Cell biology]] [[Category:Cancer]]

page.title: Talk:Apoptosis
page.text: This is a talk page and should be skipped entirely. [[Category:Cancer]]
